pageTimings.text: Fern had one small seed. She planted it in the soft ground near the Hollow Oak.
She said a growing spell. Just a small one. Just to help it along.
The next morning, the plant had grown so much it filled the Hollow Oak doorway. Leaves were coming out of every window. "Oh," said Fern.
She tried a slowing spell. The plant kept growing. She tried a stopping spell. The plant kept growing.
By afternoon, the plant had grown through the roof. It was still growing. Everyone stood back and watched.
Luna was inside. "Fern," said Luna, through a curtain of leaves, "do something, please."
Fern sat down and thought. Spells had made it grow. But a growing thing needed something different.
She did not use a spell. She sang to it, very softly. She sang the song her mother had taught her about seeds.
The plant stopped growing. The leaves curled gently inward. Then, slowly, it began to shrink.
By evening, it was a large, beautiful plant beside the Hollow Oak. Not too small. Not too large. Luna came out through the door.
Luna smiled. "That is quite nice, actually," said Luna. Fern smiled. She was very tired.
"How did you make it stop?" asked Pip. "I didn't make it do anything," said Fern. "I asked it nicely."
